TWEET 1
$650 billion. That is what Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft will spend on AI infrastructure in 2026 alone — up 60% from last year. Bridgewater just confirmed the numbers. The implications for every enterprise tech buyer are enormous.

---

TWEET 2
Bridgewater co-CIO Greg Jensen calls this a "more dangerous phase" — exponential physical infrastructure spending funded increasingly by outside capital. Translation: the hyperscalers are betting everything on AI becoming the default compute layer.

---

TWEET 3
What this means downstream: massively expanded compute capacity will push AI inference costs down significantly over the next 12-18 months. Enterprise AI deployment just got cheaper for everyone outside the Big Four.

---

TWEET 4
The risk executives should watch: when capex scales this fast, the pressure to monetize intensifies. Expect aggressive bundling, lock-in tactics, and shifting pricing models from every major cloud provider. Negotiate terms now.

For manufacturing, financial services, healthcare — sectors still evaluating AI ROI — this spending wave creates a narrow window. Infrastructure is being built. The cost of waiting is rising faster than the cost of deploying.

## Fact-check notes
- Bridgewater Associates analysis reported by Reuters on Feb 23, 2026: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft expected to collectively invest ~$650 billion in AI-related infrastructure in 2026 (Source: Reuters)
- This represents a ~60% increase from $410 billion in combined AI infrastructure spending in 2025 (Source: Reuters, Bridgewater Associates letter to clients)
- Bridgewater co-CIO Greg Jensen stated the AI boom has entered a 'more dangerous phase' marked by exponentially rising investments in physical infrastructure and growing reliance on outside capital (Source: Reuters)
